_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for execution policy. UI and CLI both read this — neither hardcodes stage rules independently. Editing this file changes system behavior everywhere.
_meta.evidence_basis: Trust classifications below are derived from real testing in this project: 3 models (llama3:8b, llama3.1:8b, deepseek-r1:14b) run against identical JD/resume/prompts. See ORCHESTRATION_SUMMARY.md for full evidence. This is not a theoretical policy — every classification traces to an observed failure or success.

authoritative.label: Verified (Claude.ai manual upload)
authoritative.description: Human reviewed the output after running it through Claude.ai. The system cannot mark anything authoritative automatically — this label only applies after a human confirms it.

advisory.label: Draft (local model — may be incorrect)
advisory.description: Local model output, on a stage with confirmed-adequate reliability. Treat as a starting point, not a final answer. Spot-check against the source resume/JD before acting on it.

unsafe.label: Do not use without review
unsafe.description: Local model output on a stage with confirmed unreliable behavior (fabrication, hallucinated entities, ignored task format). 3/3 tested models failed these stages. Never present this as a final answer in any UI.

stage_0_1_variant_rank.description: Fit gate + ranking across all resume variants against a JD
stage_0_1_variant_rank.task_shape: multi-document (up to 21 full resumes + JD in one prompt, ~52K tokens observed)
stage_0_1_variant_rank.evidence: 3/3 tested models failed: never named correct number of variants (llama3.1:8b: 0 of 21 named; deepseek-r1:14b: only 3 of 21 addressed, invented 2 non-existent variants). Larger/slower model did not improve quality (738s vs ~300s, still wrong).
ui_must_show: Stage requires manual Claude.ai review — local execution is disabled by default
If user overrides: explicit confirmation step naming the specific failure modes observed, not a generic warning

stage_1_5_prepare_variant.description: Isolates chosen variant + JD in output/, archives the rest
stage_1_5_prepare_variant.task_shape: deterministic file operation, no LLM involved
stage_1_5_prepare_variant.evidence: No model involved — pure file move logic, tested directly against mock and real data.

stage_2_ats_optimize.description: Full ATS score for one resume against one JD
stage_2_ats_optimize.evidence: All 3 models produced directionally reasonable scores and grounded strengths. 2/3 models (llama3:8b, llama3.1:8b) fabricated one Critical Gap each (incorrectly claiming the candidate lacks core PM experience). deepseek-r1:14b was accurate on this stage with no fabricated claims, but 3x slower (~870s vs ~280s).
ui_must_show: Default trust level: advisory, not authoritative
Recommend spot-checking the Critical Gaps section specifically — this is where 2/3 models fabricated content

stage_3_ats_recommend.description: File-level, line-level paraphrase edit recommendations for one resume
stage_3_ats_recommend.task_shape: multi-section (single resume but all 9 chunks + full instructions, ~6-24K tokens observed)
stage_3_ats_recommend.evidence: 3/3 tested models ignored the actual task (quote exact text, propose minimal paraphrase, cite chunk filename). Instead produced: a formatting/style audit (deepseek-r1:14b), a date-overlap audit (llama3.1:8b), neither resembling the requested output. All three referenced filenames/sections that don't exist in the actual chunk inventory.
ui_must_show: Stage requires manual Claude.ai review — local execution is disabled by default
If user overrides: explicit confirmation step naming the specific failure modes observed, not a generic warning

cover_letter.description: Cover letter draft for one resume against one JD
cover_letter.evidence: Never tested against a local model in this project. Defaults to manual_only until evidence exists — do not assume Stage 2's reliability transfers to a different task shape without testing.
ui_must_show: This stage has not been evaluated against any local model — manual-only until tested

tested_models.name: llama3:8b
tested_models.note: Fabricated one Critical Gap. Fast (~280s observed).

tested_models.name: llama3.1:8b
tested_models.note: Same fabrication pattern as llama3:8b. Fast (~280s observed).

tested_models.name: deepseek-r1:14b
tested_models.note: No fabricated claims, accurate grounded gaps. Slow (~870s observed) — reasoning-style model generates extended internal output.

stage_2_ats_optimize.recommendation: deepseek-r1:14b if time allows; llama3.1:8b if speed matters more than the marginal quality gain. Either way: advisory trust level only, never authoritative without human review.

cost_and_limits._meta: Populated only for providers actually integrated. Currently Ollama-only (local, no per-token cost). Cloud provider fields are placeholders for if/when cloud integration is added — DO NOT treat these as live until a provider is actually wired in and the values are verified against real billing.

ollama.cost_model: local compute only — no API cost, but real cost in time/electricity/device wear not tracked here
ollama.context_limits_source: queried live from /api/tags per model, never hardcoded

claude_api.cost_model: NOT YET INTEGRATED — do not display cost estimates to users until this is wired in and verified against real Anthropic billing/pricing pages

openai_api.cost_model: NOT YET INTEGRATED — same caveat as claude_api

default_behavior.principle: Clicking 'Run' must never silently execute on an unverified path. The default action is always the SAFEST option for that stage, defined per-stage above as default_action_on_run_click.

values.generate_prompt_file_only: Produces the prompt file and stops. Shows the user a clear next step: upload to Claude.ai. No model is called.
values.execute_immediately: Safe to run with no gating — deterministic, no LLM involved.
values.prompt_user_local_or_cloud: Shows an explicit choice (not a pre-selected default toggle) between local and cloud, with the trust-level and cost implications of each visible before the user picks.
